LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT SELBY AND HUDSON CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE COURT DID NOT CONSIDER THE DECLARATION OF MILES B. COOPER OR THE FACTS STATED IN THE OPPOSITION THAT WENT BEYOND THE FOUR CORNERS OF THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ("SAC"). (SEE BLANK V. KIRWAN (1985) 39 CAL.3D 311, 318.) THE SAC ADEQUATELY PLEADS FACTS TO GIVE RISE TO AN ALTER EGO THEORY. (SAC, 8-11.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SELBY AND HUDSON CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. WHETHER PLAINTIFF CAN ESTABLISH ALTER EGO/ENTERPRISE THEORY OF LIABILITY AGAINST DEFENDANT SELBY AND HUDSON CORPORATION IS A FACTUAL ISSUE. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT WHETHER DEFENDANT IS LIABLE AS AN ALTER EGO OF NEW DESOTO AND/OR WHETHER PLAINTIFF CAN ESTABLISH AN ENTERPRISE THEORY OF LIABILITY. THE COURT GRANTED PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE. THE EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS WERE OVERRULED. THE COURT DID NOT CONSIDER EXHIBIT 18 IN THIS RULING. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/MJM)

Prevailing party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Teanna L. Ward, CSR #11918 =302/eg

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-12-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MILES B. COOPER OF THE EMISON HULLVERSON LLP, TEL. NO. 434-2111 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. SCOTT E. ATKINSON & ATTY. DAVID M. KING OF CARR MCCLELLAN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (650) 342-9600 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT SELBY & HUDSON.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT SELBY AND HUDSON CORPORATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. WHETHER PLAINTIFF CAN ESTABLISH ALTER EGO/ENTERPRISE THEORY OF LIABILITY AGAINST DEFENDANT SELBY AND HUDSON CORPORATION IS A FACTUAL ISSUE. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT WHETHER DEFENDANT IS LIABLE AS AN ALTER EGO OF NEW DESOTO AND/OR WHETHER PLAINTIFF CAN ESTABLISH AN ENTERPRISE THEORY OF LIABILITY. THE COURT GRANTED PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE. THE EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS WERE OVERRULED. THE COURT DID NOT CONSIDER EXHIBIT 18 IN THIS RULING. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order
pursuant to CRC 3.1312. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/MJM)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-10-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOHN E. HULLVERSON OF EMISON/HULLVERSON LLP, TEL. NO. 434-2111 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. BILL FRIMEL, TEL. NO. (650) 996-0414 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SELBY AND HUDSON CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled. The Court did not consider the Declaration of Miles B. Cooper or the facts stated in the Opposition that went beyond the four corners of the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC"). (See Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) The SAC adequately pleads facts to give rise to an alter ego theory. (SAC, 8-11.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
